message:
I've become *that person* — the one with the slightly-too-organized closet shelf, the labeled bins, the printed copies of important docs in a folder. My family used to laugh at me. Then a water main broke on our street last month, and suddenly I wasn't the joke anymore — I was the person calmly handing out bottles and having a copy of our insurance info ready.

Honestly? It felt good. Not smug (okay, maybe a *little* smug), but mostly just... calm. Knowing we had what we needed meant I could actually focus on helping neighbors instead of spiraling.

Preparedness doesn't have to be complicated or intense. It's really just about having the basics — some water, important papers in one spot, maybe a bag with essentials — so that when something *does* go sideways, you're not starting from zero. Your future self will thank you, and you might even surprise your family with how steady you can be.

What's one thing you've prepped for that actually made a difference when you needed it?
